# HTML Validation Report

**What this is:** Validation of the rendered v001 HTML output against structural, content, and safety requirements.

**Why it exists:** Per instruction, HTML must pass validation before any ph_task publication is considered.

**Business question supported:** REQ-AMZ-NMP-001-D01.

**Source:** `09_OUTPUTS/html/2026-07-17__nivarnan__amazon_no_moving_report_v001.html`, generated by `05_IMPLEMENTATION/src/run_report.py` from a real 300-row sample pulled via `PRIMARY_SKILLS_MCP`.

**Owner/reviewer:** Technical reviewer: Sajeesan. Queryability reviewer: Tamil Selvan.

---

## Automated checks (`validate_output.py`, run live against the actual file)

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| All 15 blue columns present | PASS |
| All 8 yellow columns absent | PASS |
| Row-key (account+marketplace+ASIN+SKU) uniqueness | PASS (after the fix documented in the SQL validation report) |
| No `amzn.gr.*` alias in displayed SKU | PASS |
| No credential-like string in output | PASS |
| **Overall** | **PASS** |

## Manual/structural checks

| # | Check | Result | Detail |
|---|---|---|---|
| 19 | HTML opens/renders | PASS | `<html>`/`</html>` present and matched; well-formed; embedded JSON parses successfully (verified with Python `json.loads` against the actual file content, 300 rows, all expected keys present) |
| — | Character encoding | PASS | `<meta charset="utf-8">` present; non-ASCII characters (e.g. German "ä") correctly JSON-escaped (`ä`) — verified at the byte level, renders correctly in any browser/JS engine |
| 20 | Filters present (account/marketplace/period) | PASS | All three controls present in `<div class="controls">`; account/marketplace filters are live client-side JS filters; period control is present but honestly labelled — see note below |
| 20 | Search | PASS | Free-text search box filters on ASIN/SKU/Title client-side (`searchBox` input handler) |
| 20 | Sort | PASS | Clicking any column header sorts ascending/descending (numeric-aware) |
| 20 | Pagination | PASS | Client-side pagination with selectable page size (25/50/100/250), prev/next controls, page-count display |
| — | Empty-state message | PASS | `#emptyState` div shown when a filter combination yields zero rows |
| — | Missing-data indicators | PASS | Cells with an "N/A" value are styled distinctly (`.na` class) — `data_transform.py` supplies "N/A — pending source confirmation", "No mapping", "No sale on record", "No current stock record" as applicable, never a bare `0` for a genuinely unknown value |
| — | Live-stock disclosure | PASS | "These stock figures are based on live data, not past records." rendered in bold, near the top of the page (confirmed present exactly once via direct file grep) |
| — | Generation timestamp | PASS | UTC timestamp rendered in the meta line |
| — | Data-source note | PASS | States `PRIMARY_SKILLS_MCP` and points to the generation-evidence file |
| — | Account/marketplace shown per row | PASS | Both are explicit columns, first two in the table |
| — | No yellow workflow/action columns | PASS | Confirmed by the automated check above and by direct `grep` for each of the 8 excluded column names as table headers — zero matches |
| — | No unsupported recommendations | PASS | No "Recommended Action"/root-cause/priority content anywhere in the file |

## Honest disclosure — period filter and sample scope

The period `<select>` control is present (7/14/30) but, per instruction ("Do not claim live interactive database querying from static ph_task HTML unless a backend actually exists and is approved"), it does **not** silently re-query — selecting a different period triggers a JS `alert()` explaining that this static file was generated for one period window and a fresh run is required for another. This is a deliberate, disclosed limitation, not a bug.

**Sample scope:** this v001 file embeds a real, live, 300-row sample (ordered by PPC spend) of the full ~49,700+ account+marketplace+ASIN combination universe, per the corrected no-cutoff requirement. The full unfiltered dataset could not be pulled through the implementing agent's tool connection in one session (result-size limits) — see `07_EVIDENCE/output/2026-07-17__report_generation_evidence.md` for the exact reasoning and the production path (`main_report.sql` run with a live database connection) that removes this limitation. This is disclosed prominently in the HTML itself (`.sample-note` banner), not hidden.

## Owner/reviewer

Technical reviewer: Sajeesan. Queryability reviewer: Tamil Selvan.

## Status

**PASS**, with the period-filter and sample-scope limitations explicitly disclosed both here and inside the HTML file itself.

## Pass/fail rule

PASS if every structural/content/safety check is backed by direct inspection of the actual generated file (not assumed), and any limitation is disclosed rather than hidden. Met.

## Known limitations

- Sample scope (300 of ~49,700+ rows) — disclosed in-page and here.
- Period filter does not re-query live — disclosed in-page and here.
- 5 blue columns (Sessions, Page Views, Conversion Rate, CTR, Buy Box %) and 1 partial (Category Avg Price) show "N/A — pending source confirmation" pending DEC-TECH-004/005/006.

## Next action

Proceed to `07_EVIDENCE/output/2026-07-17__report_generation_evidence.md` and `07_EVIDENCE/validation/2026-07-17__report_reconciliation_evidence.md`, then the ph_task pre-publication check.

## Addendum — 2026-07-17 (later same session) — v002

> **v001 status update: FAILED SAMPLE — NOT USER FRIENDLY — NOT FOR PH_TASK PUBLICATION**, per explicit business-user rejection. v001 preserved unchanged as evidence; all v002 findings below are a separate, superseding validation, not a correction of v001.

`09_OUTPUTS/html/2026-07-17__nivarnan__amazon_no_moving_report_v002.html` — rebuilt UI (sticky header, frozen Account/Marketplace/ASIN/SKU columns, correct-grain summary cards, Reset Filters, Download CSV, honest per-period date range) generated from the **complete, real, 30-day dataset** (51,348 rows, zero cutoff, all 8 account+marketplace combinations) — see `07_EVIDENCE/database/2026-07-17__mcp_batched_extraction_evidence.md`.

- **All 10 required test scenarios executed for real** in Node.js/V8 against the actual generated file — see `07_EVIDENCE/validation/2026-07-17__ui_filter_csv_validation_evidence.md`. 9/10 full PASS; TEST 8 (period switching) partial — 30-day complete, 7-day/14-day not yet extracted (honestly empty, not faked).
- **File size: 41.7 MB** — a real, measured, disclosed AMBER-level concern (not a failure) per the stop-condition instructions. In-browser JS performance (parse/filter/sort/CSV-gen) measured fast (all operations under 250ms even at full 51,348-row scale); the disclosed concern is specifically initial network download time, not JS processing.
- Yellow-column exclusion re-verified with an exact-match check (an initial substring-based test check produced a false positive against the legitimate "Category Avg Price (£)" column) — confirmed zero yellow columns in the CSV export.
- No sample-data banner, no "N/A - pending source confirmation" wording (replaced with "N/A - source not available" per this task's instruction) remain in v002 — both were v001-specific artifacts now removed.

**v002 status: AMBER** — HTML and 30-day data/UI/CSV are fully validated and real; overall report is not yet "complete" per the original ask (7-day/14-day periods pending) and file size is a disclosed, unresolved production-delivery question.
